sections.paragraph: Ch. to go, a form softened from הָלַךְ, comp. דָּרַשׁ and דּוּשׁ, אָלַץ and אוּץ, עָנַק and עוּק, and in the more modern languages, Engl. talk, walk, dark, warm (in which the r is omitted in pronunciation [probably it was intended to refer to the r in the two former words;-the two latter are wholly misplaced in this comparison]). The French doux, from dulcis; faux from falsus. Fut. (the only instance of fut. A. in verbs עו֞) יְהַךְ Ezr 5:5 6:5 7:13 . Infin. מְהָךְ Ezr 7:13.
